Layout Principles:
* Put reading-first content in the left/main column: introductions, descriptions, markdown, homework content, and comments.
* Put structured facts in the right/side column: identifiers, metadata, dates, counts, actions, mini calendars, and quick links.
* On mobile, stack in that order.
* Reuse existing grids, cards, borders, muted text, tabs, and buttons.
* Popups with discussion use details on the left and discussion on the right on desktop; stack on mobile.
List Table:
* Used for discovery-type lists such as courses, sections, and teachers.
* Primary display information is placed in the first visual column.
* Structured fields serve disambiguation and comparison.
* Rows are fully clickable and navigate to the detail page.
* At mobile widths, one prominent search stays in the first viewport, advanced filters move into an accessible sheet, active filters remain removable above results, and result cards stay within the viewport without page-level horizontal scrolling.
* Paginated discovery lists expose URL-driven Previous, numbered-page, and Next links while preserving active filters.
Detail Hero:
* Used at the top of course, section, and teacher detail pages.
* Contains breadcrumb, h1, and an optional subtitle.
* h1 uses the primary display name of the current object, not an internal ID.
Model Property Priority: Properties are ranked as primary, secondary, or tertiary so web, REST, and MCP surfaces present task-relevant information consistently.
Model Property Priority - Primary: Primary properties identify the object or answer the immediate task: display title/name, distinguishing code, due/departure/start time, status, and primary action state. Put them in h1, card titles, the first list column, or the first line of compact assistant output.
Model Property Priority - Secondary: Secondary properties support disambiguation and comparison: localized secondary name, semester, campus/location, teacher/department, counts, and short metadata. Use muted text, badges, side columns, or compact nested summaries.
Model Property Priority - Tertiary: Tertiary properties support audit or exact reconstruction: internal IDs, raw timestamps, platform metadata, long nested relations, and repeated parent objects. Hide or de-emphasize them in ordinary UI; MCP default output may omit, redact, or compact them.
Model Property Priority - Surfaces: REST may expose fuller records for application clients. MCP default output preserves primary and important secondary fields, simplifies repeated low-priority fields, and reserves exact raw payloads for explicit full mode where supported; the deprecated summary input is an alias for default.
Basic Info Card:
* Used in detail page sidebars or collapsible panels.
* Displays structured fields; does not carry comment or homework interactions.
Context Tabs:
* Used for multi-context switching within detail pages.
* Common combinations: sections — homework, calendar, comments; courses — sections, comments; teachers — sections, comments.
Calendar Export Dialog:
* Used to display single-object iCal and personal subscription iCal.
* Presented as a copyable link; users are not required to manually construct the URL.
Shell Layout:
* Desktop shell uses independent scroll regions: the sidebar navigation scrolls separately from the main content column.
* Shell density favors dashboard-style spacing: compact topbar, compact sidebar links, and tighter page padding.
* Theme selection is explicit through a dropdown menu with system, light, and dark radio choices.
Dashboard Page Identity:
* Every signed-in dashboard branch exposes exactly one localized level-one heading.
* The main content landmark uses the same localized branch label.
* The document title identifies the active branch before the Life@USTC suffix.
* Identity follows the normalized dashboard tab across root, path, and query-parameter aliases.
Localized Count Copy: * User-visible English count copy distinguishes singular from plural forms; Chinese may share one count template when its grammar does not change.
Navigation Landmarks:
* The application sidebar is a real navigation landmark labelled Primary navigation / 主导航.
* Footer links use one distinct Footer navigation / 页脚导航 landmark so assistive technology can distinguish the two regions.
